:::note[Unverified storyline]
Community-derived progression adapted to this shard; creature stats and creation rules are
cross-checked against source-verified wiki pages, but the route itself awaits field
verification. File discrepancies per [wiki conventions](/guides/wiki-conventions/).
:::

The warrior is the cheapest, most forgiving way to learn the shard: swing a katana, apply a
bandage, loot the corpse. No reagent bills, no pet logistics — your only running costs are
bandages and armor repair.

**Endgame goal (700.0 total):** [Swordsmanship](/skills/swordsmanship/),
[Tactics](/skills/tactics/), Anatomy, [Healing](/skills/healing/), Resisting Spells,
[Magery](/skills/magery/), Meditation — all at 100. Magery is utility (Recall, Greater Heal),
not your damage; the first four skills are the build's spine.

## Character creation on this shard

Full rules: [character creation](/mechanics/character-creation/) (90 stat points, 10–60 per
stat; 4 skills, 50.0 max each, exactly 120 total).

- **Stats: 55 STR / 25 DEX / 10 INT.** STR is hit points and damage; DEX is swing and
  bandage speed; keep INT at the floor — [stat gain](/mechanics/stat-gain/) fixes it later.
- **Skills: Swordsmanship 50, Tactics 50, Healing 20.** Each katana-granting skill stacks:
  Swords and Tactics each grant a katana, Healing adds **50 bandages + scissors**. An
  alternative split — Swords 50, Healing 50, Anatomy 20 — buys Healing's 60/60 cure
  threshold sooner at the cost of early damage; take it only if you expect to fight
  poisoners early.
- **City: [Britain](/world/britain/).** Every service, the busiest bank, and the graveyard
  at (1380, 1490) is your Stage 1 hunting ground. [Trinsic](/world/trinsic/) is the
  thematic warrior alternative (smith, healer, walls), but its escalation path leads toward
  Destard, which you will not be ready for soon. [Jhelom](/world/jhelom/) has the best
  weapon/armor vendor cluster if you prefer a quiet start.
- You also get the universal kit: 1,000 gold, dagger, candle, red book.

First purchases (~300–500 gold): a full suit of NPC armor from the blacksmith, 100+ extra
bandages (buy cloth/bandages from healer or tailor), and a few spare katanas.

## Stage 1 — Novice (creation → ~60 weapon skill)

**Goals:** Swords/Tactics to 60, Healing past 30, Anatomy started (buy 30+ from an NPC
warrior trainer when you have spare gold — it is direct damage and feeds Healing).

- Warm up on town-edge vermin: [giant rats](/bestiary/monsters/giant-rat/) (26–39 hits),
  [mongbats](/bestiary/monsters/mongbat/) (4–6). A few minutes only — they stop teaching
  fast. Era advice says training dummies cap out around 25 skill; you start at 50, so skip
  them entirely.
- Move to the **Britain graveyard**: [skeletons](/bestiary/undead/skeleton/) (34–48 hits,
  3–7 dmg), [zombies](/bestiary/undead/zombie/) (28–42), then
  [ghouls](/bestiary/undead/ghoul/) (46–60). Bandage between fights — Healing trains while
  you train.
- When graveyard fights feel safe, walk the roads for
  [brigands](/bestiary/humanoids/brigand/) (10–23 dmg — respect them; they swing like
  players) and [ettins](/bestiary/humanoids/ettin/) (82–99 hits, 7–17 dmg).

**Collect:** all gold, gems, weapons/armor with magic properties, bone armor pieces from
skeletons. **Money:** graveyard spawn carries Poor/Meager loot — expect pocket change; the
real Stage 1 income is brigands and ettins (Average-tier packs, potions on ettins).

## Stage 2 — Journeyman (60 → 90)

**Goals:** weapon/Tactics to 90, Healing+Anatomy both to **80/80** — that unlocks bandage
**resurrection** ([Healing](/skills/healing/)), the single biggest survivability upgrade in
the build. Start buying Resisting Spells up and let it gain against casters.

- **Despise** — on this shard its roster is ettins, ogres, and earth elementals
  ([dungeons](/world/dungeons/)): [ogres](/bestiary/humanoids/ogre/) (100–117 hits, club +
  potions in loot), then [earth elementals](/bestiary/elementals/earth-elemental/)
  (76–93 hits, 9–16 dmg, 100% physical — your armor's favorite enemy; they carry gems and
  mandrake root and will take you to GM swords eventually).
- **Covetous** — [harpies](/bestiary/monsters/harpy/) (58–72 hits, easy, **50 feathers
  each** — sellable in bulk) up top; liches below are Stage 3 material.
- **Deceit** — undead density; [bone knights](/bestiary/undead/bone-knight/) and
  [skeletal mages](/bestiary/undead/skeletal-mage/). Era guides recommend silver weapons
  against undead here; whether silver bonuses apply on this shard is **unverified** — test
  and report.
- **Wrong** — mixed spawn per the [dungeons page](/world/dungeons/);
  [trolls](/bestiary/humanoids/troll/) (106–123 hits) and
  [frost trolls](/bestiary/humanoids/frost-troll/) are era-typical here but unverified.

**Collect:** gems (small, dense value), magic weapons/armor (properties are visible on this
AoS-rules shard — no identification step), potions. **Money:** ettin/ogre/elemental farming
funds your first horse (era price ~550 gold at a stable — unverified here), a runebook, and
GM-quality armor. Sell vendor-grade magic loot to NPCs, keep anything with good properties
to trade with players at the [Britain](/world/britain/) bank.

## Stage 3 — Master (90+)

**Goals:** GM weapon/Tactics/Anatomy/Healing; Resist climbing against real casters; start
Magery/Meditation from scrolls and NPC training for Recall mobility
([Recall](/magic/circle-4/recall/) is *not* craftable by you — buy scrolls).

- Earth elementals to GM, then escalate: [ogre lords](/bestiary/humanoids/ogre-lord/)
  (476–552 hits, Rich ×2 loot), Covetous/Deceit [liches](/bestiary/undead/lich/) (103–120
  hits but 24–26 dmg spells — this is what Resist is for), then
  [lich lords](/bestiary/undead/lich-lord/) (250–303 hits, FilthyRich loot, necro scrolls).
- **Destard** ([drakes](/bestiary/monsters/drake/) 241–258,
  [dragons](/bestiary/monsters/dragon/) 478–495, FilthyRich ×2 + 8 gems each) and
  **Khaldun** are group content — bring a mage or tamer friend, or go in Felucca for
  better loot if you accept PvP ([server rules](/shard/server-rules/)).

## The trade loop

| Buy | Where |
|---|---|
| Armor, weapons, repairs | [Britain](/world/britain/) / [Trinsic](/world/trinsic/) / [Jhelom](/world/jhelom/) blacksmiths |
| Bandages, cloth | Healers and tailors, any full-service city |
| Potions | Alchemists ([Moonglow](/world/moonglow/), Britain) |
| Recall/Gate scrolls, Magery training | Mage shops (Britain, [Moonglow](/world/moonglow/), [Vesper](/world/vesper/)) |

| Sell | Where |
|---|---|
| Vendor-grade magic loot, gems | Any NPC jeweler/provisioner |
| Feathers (harpies), bone armor | Bowyers / players |
| Good magic gear | Players at the Britain bank or the [forum trade board](https://www.uotavern.com/forum) |

## Decision points & common mistakes

- **If your Healing+Anatomy are below 60/60**, you cannot cure poison with bandages — avoid
  poisoning spawn or carry cure potions.
- **If a target casts spells (liches, evil mages) and your Resist is under ~50**, walk away;
  era wisdom and our lich damage data (24–26 per hit) agree it ends badly.
- **If you are dying faster than you loot**, drop one rung down the ladder — gains barely
  slow, income steadies ([skill gain](/mechanics/skill-gain/) rewards "challenging", not
  "suicidal").
- **If you cross into Felucca** for loot bonuses, bank first and carry only what you can
  lose.
- **Mistake:** skipping Healing/Anatomy "for more damage." 80/80 bandage-res turns dungeon
  deaths from disasters into pauses.
- **Mistake:** fighting dragons or liches before Resist. Your plate armor does nothing
  against an energy bolt.
- **Mistake:** hoarding loot mid-trip. Gems and gold are light, but armor adds up — bank in
  town between dungeon runs.
